meta.title: Don't Forget the Motor City: Philanthropy in Detroit index.c
meta.description:
American philanthropy claims to have responded generously to the plea not to "forget the Motor City" famously put by Martha and the Vandellas in their Motown hit from 1964, "Dancing in the Street." As a recent cover story by the Chronicle of Philanthropy's Suzanne Perry found, "grant makers from across the country have provided more than $628 million dollars in the Motor City in the past few years."
